title: Examining Perceptual Grouping on Stages of Processing in Visual Working Memory: An ERP Study
description: This EEG dataset comprises electrophysiological recordings from 53 subjects performing a bilateral change-detection task designed to investigate how perceptual grouping based on color repetition influences visual working memory (VWM) processing. Using event-related potentials (ERPs), the study temporally isolates neural correlates of distinct VWM stages—individuation encoding, maintenance, initial comparison, percept-memory comparison, and decision making—through specific ERP markers (N2pc, CDA, N2, FN400). The dataset includes 1,248 trials recorded at 512 Hz from a 71-channel BioSemi ActiveTwo system and is structured according to BIDS standards.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a BioSemi ActiveTwo system with 71 channels (64 scalp, 3 EOG, 2 mastoid, 1 CMS/DRL reference) at a sampling rate of 512 Hz. Subjects performed a bilateral change-detection task with memory arrays containing 2-3 colored squares in one visual hemifield, followed by a maintenance period and a single-item probe for change detection. Experimental conditions manipulated array composition (unrepeated vs. repeated colors) and spatial proximity of repeated items. Offline preprocessing included re-referencing to average mastoids, ICA-based artifact removal, and 0.1 Hz high-pass filtering. ERP components were measured in specific time windows relative to stimulus and probe onsets.
